extensionQuestions.question: State the orbital overlap involved in the C-H bond of ethene.
Describe the types of orbital overlap that form the carbon-carbon double bond in ethene.
extensionQuestions.answer: The double bond consists of a σ bond formed by the head-on overlap of sp² hybridised orbitals, and a π bond formed by the side-on overlap of unhybridised p orbitals.

extensionQuestions.question: How many unhybridised p orbitals remain on each carbon atom in ethyne, and what type of bonds do they form?
extensionQuestions.answer: Two unhybridised p orbitals remain on each carbon atom, and they form two π bonds via side-on overlap.

extensionQuestions.question: What does the delocalisation of π electrons refer to in terms of orbital overlap?
extensionQuestions.answer: It refers to π electrons that are free to move across multiple atoms due to the continuous side-on overlap of adjacent p-orbitals.

extensionQuestions.question: State the condition required for the delocalisation of π electrons to occur across a system of atoms.
extensionQuestions.answer: There must be continuous side-on overlap of p-orbitals over at least 3 adjacent atoms.

extensionQuestions.question: What structural features should you look for to identify if an atom has a p-orbital available for delocalisation?
extensionQuestions.answer: Look for the presence of a π bond, a lone pair of electrons, or an incomplete octet on the atom.

cards.title: ability to delocalise
cards.extensionFile: extension question/board-01 ability-to-delocalise ability to delocalise.md

extensionQuestions.question: In the molecule CH₂=CH-CH=NH, in which orbital does the lone pair of electrons on the nitrogen atom reside?
extensionQuestions.answer: The lone pair of electrons on the nitrogen atom resides in an sp² hybridised orbital.

extensionQuestions.question: Explain why the lone pair of electrons on the nitrogen atom in CH₂=CH-CH=NH is unable to delocalise into the adjacent π system.
extensionQuestions.answer: The lone pair is in an sp² orbital, which does not overlap continuously with the p-orbitals of the adjacent carbon atoms because it lies in a different plane (perpendicular to the π system).

extensionQuestions.question: Describe the orbital overlap that allows delocalisation to occur in the formate ion, HCOO⁻.
extensionQuestions.answer: The p-orbitals of the carbon atom and both oxygen atoms overlap continuously side-on.
